question_text: Why are trigonometric ratios useful in practical situations?
option_a: They relate angles to side lengths in right-angled triangles
option_b: They work only for circles and never for triangles
option_c: They replace all measurement with estimation
option_d: They apply only when every side length is known already
explanation: Trigonometric ratios connect side lengths and angles in right-angled triangles.
